Package coxprogramming.processing.sprites

Class Block

Object
Sprite
Blob
Block
public class Block extends Blob
A rectangular sprite with a width and a height.

Block extends Blob, which means it has all of Blob's movement and collision features, but draws itself as a rectangle instead of a circle. It is useful for walls, platforms, floors, and other box-shaped objects in a game.

The position (x, y) marks the top-left corner of the rectangle, following Processing's default rect() behavior.

Example: 

   Block wall = new Block(this, 0, 200, 800, 20, color(100));
   // Creates a dark-grey horizontal platform at y=200, spanning the screen.

  • Field Details

    • w

      protected float w
      The width of the rectangle in pixels. Always positive.

    • h

      protected float h
      The height of the rectangle in pixels. Always positive.

  • Constructor Details

    • Block

      public Block(PApplet parent, float x, float y, float w, float h, int c)
      Creates a stationary Block. Negative values for width or height are automatically made positive.
      Parameters:
      parent - your Processing sketch â€” pass this
      x - the x position of the top-left corner
      y - the y position of the top-left corner
      w - the width of the rectangle in pixels
      h - the height of the rectangle in pixels
      c - the fill color â€” use Processing's color() function

  • Method Details

    • drawSprite

      public void drawSprite()
      Draws this Block as a filled rectangle with no outline. Call this once per frame inside your draw() loop.
      Overrides:
      drawSprite in class Blob
